author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
vibhāvarī⋅śeṣa, āloka praveśa,
nidrā chāḍi’ uṭha jīva
bala’ hari hari, mukunda murāri,
rāma kṛṣṇa hayagrīva

nṛsiŋha vāmana, śrī-madhusūdana,
vrajendranandana śyāma
pūtanā⋅ghātana, kaiṭabha⋅śātana,
jaya dāśarathi⋅rāma

jaśodā⋅dulāla, govinda⋅gopāla,
vṛndāvana⋅purandara
gopīpriya⋅jana, rādhikā⋅ramaṇa,
bhuvana⋅sundaravara

rāvaṇāntakara, mākhana⋅taskara,
gopī⋅jana⋅vastrahārī
vrajera rākhāla, gopa-vṛndapāla,
citta-hārī vaṁśī-dhārī

jogīndra⋅vandana, śrī⋅nanda⋅nandana,
vraja⋅jana⋅bhayahārī
navīna nīrada, rūpa manohara,
mohana⋅vaṁśīvihārī

jaśodā⋅nandana, kaṁsa⋅nisūdana,
nikuñjarāsa⋅vilāsī
kadamba⋅kānana, rāsa-parāyaṇa,
vṛndāvipina⋅nivāsī

ānanda⋅vardhana, prema⋅niketana,
phulaśara⋅yojaka kāma
gopāṅganā-gaṇa⋅, citta⋅vinodana,
samasta⋅guṇagaṇa⋅dhāma

jāmuna⋅jīvana, keli⋅parāyaṇa,
mānasacandra⋅cakora
nāma⋅sudhārasa, gāo kṛṣṇa⋅yaśa,
rākha vacana mana mora
en_translation:
The night ends and light enters; rise up, O soul, and leave your sleep. Chant the names of Hari, Mukunda, Murāri, Rāma, Kṛṣṇa, and Hayagrīva.

Chant the names of Nṛsiṁha, Vāmana, Śrī Madhusūdana, and Śyāma. All glories to the killer of Pūtanā, the destroyer of Kaiṭabha, and to Rāma, the son of Daśaratha.

He is Yaśodā's darling, Govinda, Gopāla, and the Lord of Vṛndāvana. He is the beloved of the gopīs, the lover of Rādhā, and the most beautiful in all the world.

He is the destroyer of Rāvaṇa, the butter-thief, and the one who stole the clothes of the gopīs. He is the cowherd of Vraja, the protector of the cowherds, the captivator of the mind, and the player of the flute.

He is worshiped by the great yogis—the son of Nanda who removes the fears of the residents of Vraja. Like a fresh cloud with a charming form, He is the enchanting flute-player.

He is the son of Yaśodā, the killer of Kaṁsa, and the one who enjoys the rāsa dance in the groves. In the kadamba forest, He is absorbed in the rāsa dance as a resident of the forests of Vṛndāvana.

He is the enhancer of bliss, the abode of love, and more enchanting than Cupid. He is the enchanter of the minds of the cowherd girls and the abode of all good qualities.

He is the life of the Yamunā, absorbed in pastimes—the moon for the chakora bird of the mind. O my mind, sing the glories of Kṛṣṇa, the nectar of the Holy Name, and follow these words of mine.
